bosshunt-ossuarywarden-desc:
Your target — the Ossuary Warden. This is not living flesh, but animated weight of the vaults themselves, relentless and deaf to pleas.

It's as slow as shifting slabs, but its blow crushes stone to sand. It presses with mass and uses surroundings as weapons, hurling debris from darkness.

But be careful. In the Ossuary death is a stretchable concept. What seems defeated has a nasty habit of rising again. Don't hurry to lower your shield when the rumbling stops. The true trial begins exactly when you think you've won. Save your strength — you'll need it more than once.
bosshunt-ossuarywarden-journal-text: Hunt: Ossuary Warden.\n\nMonolithic power. Main rule: don't get hit — it breaks spines. And never turn your back on it, even if it crumbled to dust.
bosshunt-ossuarywarden-reward: The Warden is defeated. The Keeper nodded into darkness: 'You destroyed the shell, then broke the will. Not bad for a mortal. But don't be fooled — this was merely a gatekeeper.'
bosshunt-ossuarywarden-reward-journal-te: The Warden finally collapsed, turning into a heap of dead debris. The echo of the blow faded, and silence became even heavier.\n\nThe Keeper didn't even look at the remains. 'The vaults remember form,' he croaked. The walls will birth a new guardian, it's inevitable. But while the passage is free, and the Bloodmeter in my hand pulses, waiting for new blood.
